commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - There is one narrow mechanical blocker: remove login/profile startup from the WSL shell bridge and update the focused test before maintainer review resumes.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a token-adjacent shell invocation that currently runs login/profile startup files before the clipboard command.; Review finding: [P2] Use a non-login shell for WSL clipboard copies — src/infra/clipboard.ts:4 (sha=f8e4fbe1aa4372563ae0e8447c06aa9b18a51508)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WSL-only clipboard backend that pipes clipboard text through bash to Windows `clip.exe` and adds a regression test that keeps the token URL out of argv.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +24. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection and the linked report: current main uses argv-based process spawning for `clip.exe`, while the dashboard token URL is supplied on stdin. I did not get live WSL2 readback proof because the PR body reports the WSL2 runner allocation failed.
commands.review_followup: - There is one narrow mechanical blocker: remove login/profile startup from the WSL shell bridge and update the focused test before maintainer review resumes.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary
- route WSL clipboard copies through a static shell bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv
- preserve existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, and PowerShell clipboard fallbacks

Fixes #88080

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main`

Behavior addressed: WSL dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct `clip.exe` argv execution, which can fail under WSL interop.

Real environment tested: local focused unit/link tests in a Codex worktree. A live WSL2 Crabbox proof was attempted with Azure provider run `run_66cf720281d5`, slug `brisk-hermit`, but no WSL2 lease was allocated after 10 minutes and `crabbox list --provider azure --json` showed no matching lease/VM.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts` plus formatter, lint, diff check, and autoreview above. The attempted WSL2 command would have run those tests plus a live `copyToClipboard` round-trip checked with `powershell.exe Get-Clipboard`.

Evidence after fix: `copyToClipboard` now tries `bash -lc 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` only when WSL is detected, and the regression test asserts the token URL is passed via stdin rather than command argv.

Observed result after fix: focused tests passed; autoreview returned no accepted/actionable findings. WSL2 runner allocation did not produce a VM, so no live WSL2 clipboard readback result was available.

What was not tested: live WSL2 clipboard round-trip on a real Windows host due to Azure WSL2 Crabbox lease allocation blocking before a VM was created.
target.author: vincentkoc
